channel.title: Brazil Elections 2026
channel.description: Real-time monitoring of the 2026 Brazilian presidential elections.

item.title: A ministros, Lula critica Flávio e diz temer inflação por crise do petróleo
item.description: President Lula criticized Flávio Bolsonaro during a farewell ministerial meeting, expressing concern over inflation caused by the oil crisis stemming from the Middle East conflict. He warned that rising prices could negatively impact the Brazilian economy.

item.title: Agente envolvido em plano para tentar matar Lula, Alckmin e Moraes é exonerado da PF
item.description: The Ministry of Justice and Public Security dismissed federal police officer Wladimir Matos Soares, who was sentenced to 21 years in prison for involvement in the 'núcleo 3' of the coup plot aimed at keeping Jair Bolsonaro in power after the 2022 election defeat. The plot included assassination attempts against President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva, Vice President Geraldo Alckmin, and Supreme Court Minister Alexandre de Moraes.

item.title: Sabatina de Messias ocorrerá no 'tempo de Davi' e não tem prazo definido ainda, diz presidente da CCJ
item.description: The sabatina of Minister Jorge Messias to the STF is likely to be delayed until the second semester, as it depends on Senate President Davi Alcolumbre’s agenda, who shows no urgency to advance the process. CCJ President Otto Alencar stated he will act only after receiving the nomination and that the timeline will follow Alcolumbre’s pace. The deadlock reflects the strained relationship between the Planalto and the Senate, with the nomination considered low priority ahead of the October municipal elections.

item.title: Ala do PSD avalia que Caiado se posicionou como plano B da direita e vê chance de Flávio murchar na campanha
item.description: A faction within the PSD views Goiás Governor Ronaldo Caiado as positioning himself as a 'Plan B' for right-wing voters by adopting Bolsonaro-aligned rhetoric, including a promise of amnesty for Jair Bolsonaro, anticipating that Flávio Bolsonaro may falter due to past controversies and internal strife within Bolsonarismo. While more centrist members of the party criticize these moves, they acknowledge the strategy as necessary to capture right-wing support should Flávio fail to withstand campaign pressures.

item.title: Os bilhões do Master e o racismo no Brasil
item.description: The article denounces that while Brazil's financial system treats billionaire losses by elites like Banco Master as administrative failures, movements for historical reparations for Black communities are criminalized as threats to stability. The Banco Master case, led by Daniel Vorcaro, involved a R$ 50 billion fraud, bribery of Central Bank officials, and coercion to evade oversight, while the state spent R$ 285 billion over 20 years on the 'war on drugs' — a racial control mechanism that incarcerates and kills predominantly Black people.

item.title: Lei reestrutura carreiras do serviço público federal
item.description: Law 15.367/2025 restructures federal public service careers, creating over 24,000 new permanent positions and benefiting more than 200,000 servants. The law introduces direct election of university rectors, incentives for technical-administrative staff in education, and establishes the Federal Institute of Sertão Paraibano, with an estimated budget impact of R$ 5.3 billion in 2026.

item.title: Ministro da Casa Civil critica comunicação do governo sob gestão de Sidônio, que reage e joga a culpa em seu antecessor
item.description: Chief of Staff Rui Costa publicly criticized the federal government's communication during a meeting with President Lula, mentioning Sidônio Palmeira four times and causing embarrassment to the Secom head. Sidônio rebutted the criticism without naming Costa, blaming the previous administration under Paulo Pimenta and citing legal restrictions on government comparisons between administrations. The incident unfolds amid Lula's 46% disapproval rating in the March Datafolha poll, with allies doubting the effectiveness of the strategy to contrast Lula's government with Bolsonaro's as a political attack tool.

item.title: Fazenda estuda pacote para reduzir inadimplência, diz ministro
item.description: Finance Minister Dario Durigan said the economic team of President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva's government is studying a package of measures to reduce household debt default in Brazil, following a request from the president. The proposals are being developed in dialogue with ministries and financial sector actors, including Febraban, which provided a diagnosis on debt concentrations in credit cards, overdrafts, and payroll loans. Household debt service reached a record 29.3% of income in January 2026, and defaults over 90 days rose to 5.24%, the highest since 2012.

item.title: Moraes manda PGR se manifestar sobre vídeo de Eduardo mostrado a Bolsonaro
item.description: Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes has ordered the Attorney General's Office (PGR) to comment on a video in which Eduardo Bolsonaro claims he is producing content to show his father, former President Jair Bolsonaro, who is barred from accessing social media. The move aims to investigate potential violations of judicial orders concerning the former president's use of digital platforms.

item.title: Lula terá mais relevância para a história do que Getúlio, diz Fernando Morais
item.description: Biographer Fernando Morais claims Lula will have greater historical relevance than Getúlio Vargas, emphasizing his journey from the 1982 defeat to the 2002 presidential victory as a process of political learning and transformation. He highlights the Citizenship Caravans as a 'post-doctorate' that deepened Lula's understanding of Brazil's inequalities, enabling him to build majorities in a context of globalization and fragmented democracy. The interview also previews the third volume of the biography, which will systematically analyze Lula's conflictual relationship with the press using the Manchetômetro.

item.title: Lula sanciona lei que amplia licença-paternidade
item.description: President Lula signed a law that gradually expands paternity leave in Brazil, increasing it from 5 to 20 days by 2029. The measure aims to increase fathers' participation in caring for newborns or adopted children, following 38 years of debate in the National Congress.

item.title: Valdemar diz que é preciso resolver os problemas da família Bolsonaro para Flávio ganhar as eleições
item.description: Valdemar Costa Neto, president of the PL, said it is necessary to resolve internal problems within the Bolsonaro family for Flávio Bolsonaro to defeat Lula in the 2026 elections. He stated he has already scheduled a meeting with Flávio to address the rift, particularly between Michelle Bolsonaro and Eduardo Bolsonaro, who lives in the U.S. and publicly criticized the stepmother’s influence on party decisions. Valdemar also advocated for a woman as vice-presidential candidate, favoring Tereza Cristina, but ruled out Michelle, calling it 'very difficult'.

item.title: Lula diz a ministros que Flávio Bolsonaro vai entregar Brasil para Trump se for eleito
item.description: President Lula stated that if elected, Flávio Bolsonaro would hand over Brazil to the United States, defending national sovereignty and labeling the adversary a 'traitor of the homeland'. The remark was made during a meeting with ministers on Tuesday, March 31, 2026.

item.title: Haddad surpreende na largada e acende alerta no entorno de Tarcísio após pesquisa
item.description: The AtlasIntel poll shows Fernando Haddad with 42.6% and Tarcísio de Freitas with 49.1% in voting intentions for the São Paulo governorship, a narrow 6.5-point gap that surprised both campaigns and indicates a more competitive race than expected. Haddad’s performance, exceeding initial PT expectations, has raised alarms in Tarcísio’s camp, undermining the perception of a comfortable lead despite his advantage. Analysts describe the race as increasingly polarized between right and center-left, likely marginalizing alternative candidates.

item.title: Homens terão que aprender a trocar fralda com nova lei, diz Lula
item.description: President Lula signed a law gradually expanding paternity leave in Brazil from 5 to 20 days, effective 2027 with full implementation by 2029. He emphasized the law’s role in teaching men to care for children, including bathing and changing diapers, as part of shifting gender roles. The legislation includes budgetary exemptions to bypass fiscal rules, allows leave to be split, guarantees job protection, and extends benefits in cases of disability or hospitalization.

item.title: Alcolumbre sabia que Lula enviaria nome de Messias em breve, mas não que seria nesta terça
item.description: Senate President Davi Alcolumbre knew Lula would soon send Jorge Messias’ nomination to the STF, but did not expect it to be delivered on Tuesday (31). Their last direct conversation on the matter occurred about 15 days ago, with no follow-up since. Alcolumbre stated the nomination is the president’s prerogative and declined to personally lead its approval, which will be handled by government leader Jaques Wagner.

item.title: Lula “perde uns votinhos” com nova regra para presos, diz Van Hattem
item.description: Federal Deputy Marcel van Hattem (Novo-RS) claimed that President Lula will lose votes due to the sanction of his amendment stripping voting rights from provisional detainees, included in the Anti-Faction Bill. The measure, now part of Law No. 15.358/2026, amends the Electoral Code to bar those in pre-trial detention from voting or retaining voter registration. The government expects the STF to rule the measure unconstitutional, avoiding political cost.

item.title: Messias diz que, com 'humildade, vai buscar 'novamente' diálogo com senadores
item.description: Messias said that, with humility, he will seek dialogue again with senators, valuing conciliation as a way to resolve conflicts. He commented on the AGU's nomination to the STF, which Lula will send to the Senate this Tuesday.

item.title: Indicação de Jorge Messias ao STF será enviada ao Senado nesta terça
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva is set to formally submit Jorge Messias’ nomination to the Supreme Federal Court (STF) to the Senate this Tuesday (31), ending a months-long delay since the announcement in November 2025. Messias, the Attorney General of the Union and a member of the Baptist Christian Church, will be evaluated by senators to join the STF’s Second Chamber, though Senate President Davi Alcolumbre has expressed preference for Rodrigo Pacheco and introduced uncertainty over the confirmation.

item.title: Caiado se reúne com seu marqueteiro
item.description: Ronaldo Caiado will have his first meeting with strategist Paulo Vasconcellos tonight after being officially launched as a presidential candidate. The meeting marks an initial step in building his campaign strategy. The article is exclusive to subscribers and requires registration for full access.

item.title: Governo Lula não indica orçamento necessário para cumprir Plano Clima
item.description: The Lula government's Climate Plan was released without a clear estimate of the budget needed to meet its targets through 2035. Experts warn that the lack of detailed financing information may hinder the implementation of planned actions, leaving them unimplemented. The absence of financial transparency raises doubts about the government's commitment to the climate agenda.

item.title: Lula confirma Alckmin como vice na chapa para as eleições 2026
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva confirmed that Vice President Geraldo Alckmin will remain on the presidential ticket for the 2026 elections, ruling out a Senate run for Alckmin. Lula stated that Alckmin will resign from the Ministry of Development, Industry, Commerce and Services by April 4 to comply with TSE electoral ineligibility rules. Although some PT factions had advocated for an MDB candidate as vice president, Alckmin insisted he would not accept any other position unless retained as Lula’s running mate.

item.title: Lula amplia licença-paternidade para até 20 dias, sem vetos
item.description: President Lula signed a law expanding paternity leave from 5 to 20 days, phased in: 10 days in 2027, 15 in 2028, and 20 in 2029, contingent on fiscal targets. The benefit, funded by Social Security, is exempt from fiscal rules and the LDO, with estimated costs of R$ 2.2 billion in 2027 rising to R$ 4.3 billion from 2029. It includes extensions for disability, hospitalization, and adoption, with job protection and right to return to the same position.

item.title: Lula encaminha indicação de Messias ao STF nesta 3ª para o Senado, diz Planalto
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva will submit on Tuesday to the Senate the nomination of Attorney General Jorge Messias for a vacancy on the Supreme Federal Court. The information was confirmed by the Palácio do Planalto.

item.title: Lula confirma que Alckmin volverá a ser su candidato a vicepresidente en las elecciones
item.description: Lula confirmed that Geraldo Alckmin will again be his vice-presidential candidate in the 2026 elections, keeping the ticket that won in 2022. The decision reflects the president's preference for stability and the functioning of the alliance between left and center-right, initially formed as a barrier to Bolsonarism. Despite being former adversaries, Lula and Alckmin maintain a partnership considered effective by the government.

item.title: Caiado enterra ‘terceira via’ e acirra disputa entre Lula e Flávio pelo eleitor de centro
item.description: Ronaldo Caiado's presidential pre-candidacy for the PSD reflects Gilberto Kassab's strategy to strengthen the party's legislative bloc rather than create a third-way alternative. Caiado positioned himself as an antipetist adversary, promising amnesty to Bolsonaro and attacking Lula, while avoiding direct confrontation with Flávio Bolsonaro. His candidacy intensifies the center-voter contest between Lula and Flávio, with Caiado likely drawing right-wing votes that could benefit Lula in the first round but also position him as a potential secondary ally to Flávio in a runoff.

item.title: Lula sanciona projeto de lei que amplia licença-paternidade para até 20 dias; veja regras
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva signed a law gradually expanding paternity leave from 5 to up to 20 days, with phased increases through 2029. The legislation also introduces a paternity salary, a social security benefit paid during leave, and includes provisional job stability after the leave. The measure, which covers self-employed workers, has an estimated cost of R$ 5.4 billion by 2030 and aims to reduce gender disparities in both work and home environments.

item.title: Saiba quem é Jorge Messias, indicado por Lula para o STF
item.description: Jorge Messias, nominated by Lula for the Supreme Federal Court (STF), is the Attorney General of the Union and a close ally of the PT, with a career defined by party loyalty and legal defense of the federal government. His background includes roles at the Central Bank, National Treasury, Ministry of Education, and legal advisory during Dilma Rousseff’s presidency, where he gained national attention due to the Lava Jato wiretaps. He played a key role in reconciling Lula and former STF President Luís Roberto Barroso and also built relationships with Bolsonaro-appointed justices such as Nunes Marques and André Mendonça.

item.title: Lula envia ao Senado ainda hoje indicação de Messias ao STF
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva will send today to the Senate the formal nomination of Jorge Messias, current head of the AGU, to fill a vacancy on the STF left by Luís Roberto Barroso. The nomination, announced in November, had caused tension with Senate President Davi Alcolumbre, who supported Rodrigo Pacheco’s candidacy. Despite strained relations, Lula opted to forward the nomination now, citing a more favorable political environment.

item.title: Governo Tarcísio tem 53% de aprovação em São Paulo, diz pesquisa
item.description: Governor Tarcísio de Freitas (Republicanos) has a 53% approval rating in São Paulo according to an AtlasIntel/Estado poll conducted March 24–29, 2026, with 45% disapproval. Approval is highest among evangelicals (74.9%), voters aged 45–59 (66.6%), men (57.5%), and residents of Presidente Prudente and Marília (68.3%). Half of respondents (50%) believe São Paulo is on the right track, and 51.3% support his reelection.

item.title: Lula destrava indicação e formaliza Messias ao STF depois de 4 meses
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva formally nominated Jorge Messias, minister of the AGU, to the Supreme Federal Court after four months of vacancy following Roberto Barroso's departure. Despite lacking the 41 Senate votes required for approval, Lula proceeded, asserting the president's exclusive prerogative to nominate and aiming to avoid timing the confirmation with the 2026 electoral calendar. Messias currently has 25 declared senatorial supporters, short of the 41 needed, and requires at least 16 additional votes to secure confirmation.

item.title: Sergio Moro responde se mudou de opinião sobre críticas a Jair Bolsonaro
item.description: Senator Sergio Moro (PL) stated he has not changed his past criticisms of Jair Bolsonaro, but is now focused on building a broader alliance to defeat the Lula government. He acknowledged that historical disagreements remain, but are secondary to current electoral priorities, justifying his alignment with the PL and Bolsonaro as a strategic move to strengthen his gubernatorial campaign in Paraná.

item.title: Lula e Caiado já se enfrentaram em eleição presidencial de 1989; veja vídeo
item.description: The governor of Goiás, Ronaldo Caiado, officially announced his pre-candidacy for the presidency under the PSD on Monday (30), reviving a presidential electoral confrontation with Lula that took place in 1989, when both ran for president. At the time, Caiado, aged 40, competed against Lula, aged 44 and the PT candidate, in that historic election.

item.title: Candidatos e governadores do PSD ignoram nas redes lançamento de Caiado à Presidência
item.description: Ronaldo Caiado, the PSD's 2026 presidential candidate, aims to end national polarization but faces internal party divisions, with factions backing Lula (PT), Flávio Bolsonaro (PL), and Romeu Zema (Novo). His official launch is threatened by lack of unity, despite his conciliatory national message.

item.title: Demora de Lula em envio do nome de Messias como indicação ao STF ultrapassa quatro meses e é recorde
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva will send the name of Jorge Messias, Attorney General of the Union, to the Senate this Tuesday for a vacancy on the Supreme Federal Court (STF), after a 130-day delay since the announcement on November 20, 2025 — a historical record. The delay was attributed to internal government assessments of insufficient votes for approval and contributed to political distancing with Senate President Davi Alcolumbre, who had previously announced a timetable that stalled. The nomination now depends on a hearing in the Constitution and Justice Committee (CCJ) and a secret vote in the plenary, requiring at least 41 votes.

item.title: Lula sanciona aumento gradual de licença-paternidade, chegando a 20 dias em 2029
item.description: President Lula signed into law a gradual expansion of paternity leave in Brazil, increasing it from 5 to 20 days by 2029, with full salary paid by the federal government through Social Security. The measure complies with an STF ruling and includes protections for adoption, same-sex couples, domestic violence cases, and the death of the mother, while ensuring job security and employer reimbursement.

item.title: A estratégia da oposição para tentar segurar PEC do fim da escala 6×1
item.description: The opposition aims to delay the PEC ending the 6×1 work schedule, seen as a potential electoral asset for Lula, by demanding compensation for businesses and lobbying to slow its progress. Despite strong public support making rejection unlikely, opponents seek to stretch the timeline, especially ahead of a possible vote in May, Labor Day month. Their goal is to prevent the PEC from becoming a positive government milestone before the 2026 elections.

item.title: Lula sanciona ampliação da licença-paternidade; veja o que muda e os próximos passos
item.description: President Lula signed into law a gradual expansion of paternity leave in Brazil, increasing it from 5 to 20 days between 2027 and 2029 for birth, adoption, or custody cases. The policy aligns paternity leave with maternity leave in specific scenarios and aims to promote gender equality and child development.

item.title: PT aposta em recall para Senado em SP, mas oposição conta com a força de Tarcísio
item.description: The PT is betting on Simone Tebet and Marina Silva — well-known figures with strong electoral recall — to win the Senate seat in São Paulo, but struggles to overcome the perception of their alignment with the Lula government, which may deter center-right voters. On the right, Governor Tarcísio de Freitas remains a powerful force, boosting allies like Guilherme Derrite, though the coalition is still fractured in choosing a second candidate due to internal disagreements.

item.title: Ministério da Justiça demite agente da PF envolvido no plano para matar Lula e Moraes
item.description: The Ministry of Justice dismissed Federal Police agent Wladimir Matos Soares, sentenced to 21 years in prison by the STF for his role in a coup plot that included the assassination of President Lula, Vice President Geraldo Alckmin, and Minister Alexandre de Moraes. Soares, who served in Lula’s security detail between his certification and inauguration in 2022, leaked sensitive security information to Bolsonaro allies, directly aiding the golpist scheme. The dismissal was ordered by Moraes, who condemned Soares for abusing his position to advance the anti-democratic intent.

item.title: Ao inaugurar monotrilho prometido para Copa de 2014, Tarcísio cita Fuleco, diz que obra atrasou por ‘má-gestão’ e promete extensão
item.description: Governor Tarcísio de Freitas inaugurated São Paulo's Line 17 (Gold) monorail, originally promised for the 2014 World Cup, after years of delay which he attributed to 'mismanagement' without naming specific politicians. He referenced the World Cup mascot Fuleco and announced the bidding process for an extension adding four new stations, framing the project as ending a legacy of 'shame' and 'white elephants' from the Cup. The line will initially operate on a limited schedule and fare-free, with plans for gradual expansion.

item.title: Lula decide enviar o nome de Messias ao Senado, diz Planalto
item.description: The Lula government will send the name of Advocacia-Geral da União Minister Jorge Messias to the Senate for a Supreme Court vacancy, after a four-month delay due to Senate President Davi Alcolumbre's preference for Rodrigo Pacheco. The move aims to unblock the formal process and restart dialogue with senators, amid concerns over the STF's vacancy during the Banco Master investigation.

item.title: Lula envia nesta terça ao Senado indicação de Messias como novo ministro do STF
item.description: President Lula has submitted to the Senate the nomination of Jorge Messias, current Attorney General of the Union, for a seat on the Supreme Federal Court (STF), filling the vacancy left by retiring Justice Luís Roberto Barroso. The nomination must be reviewed by the Constitution and Justice Committee (CCJ), undergo a public hearing, and be approved by an absolute majority (41 votes) in a secret Senate plenary vote. Messias emphasized dialogue and pacification in a social media post, following initial resistance from Senate President Davi Alcolumbre.

item.title: Caiado, Lula ou Flávio Bolsonaro? A opinião dos economistas
item.description: The market has no clear favorite candidate for the 2026 presidency but prioritizes fiscal predictability, spending control, and clear economic rules. Caiado is seen as a polarizing alternative but lacks national recognition and a detailed economic agenda. Lula holds institutional advantage but faces scrutiny over fiscal continuity, while Flávio Bolsonaro emphasizes security and institutional themes, leaving investors uncertain about his economic plan.

item.title: Governo divulga trocas em 14 ministérios; veja quem sai quem entra
item.description: The Lula government announced the replacement of 14 ministers on Tuesday (31), due to the end of the incompatibility deadline for the October elections. All newly appointed officials are current executive secretaries already working in the ministries, ensuring continuity of ongoing work. The measure complies with electoral law requiring public officials to step down six months before the election.

item.title: Kassab vence se Caiado ganhar, mas também se ele perder
item.description: House Speaker Arthur Lira stated that PSD leader Gilberto Kassab will retain political influence regardless of whether Ronaldo Caiado wins or loses in the 2026 elections. This suggests Kassab maintains significant bargaining power even if his allies face setbacks. The comment was made by journalist and social scientist Maria Inês Nassif, who emphasized Kassab’s resilience in national political alliances.

item.title: Lula reúne ministros pela última vez antes de saídas para eleições
item.description: President Lula held the final ministerial meeting before the disqualifications for the October elections, confirming that at least 18 ministers will leave the government to run for elective offices. He reaffirmed Geraldo Alckmin as his running mate and stressed the need to change the 'promiscuousness' in politics, emphasizing change through institutional means and voter action. The disqualifications must occur by April 4.

item.title: Durigan diz que adesão de estados ao subsídio do diesel está próxima da unanimidade
item.description: Finance Minister Dario Durigan stated that most Brazilian states are adopting the federal government's proposal to subsidize diesel imports, with near-unanimous support. The measure aims to lower fuel prices and curb inflation, particularly in the transportation sector.

item.title: Análise: Lula usa reunião ministerial para estrear pré-campanha com anúncio de Alckmin na vice e críticas a Bolsonaro
item.description: Lula used the ministerial meeting to launch his 2026 pre-campaign, confirming Geraldo Alckmin as his running mate and seeking to end speculation about his ticket. He reinforced a strategy of comparing his government to Bolsonaro’s, claiming the country is 'infinitely better' than four years ago, despite acknowledging it hasn’t reached a 'splendid situation'. He also instructed ministers running for office to spread this message across states, aiming to consolidate a left-wing alliance similar to 2022.

item.title: Artigo: Ranking sem transparência produz distorção e desinformação
item.description: The article criticizes the research institute ranking published by BTG Pactual and O GLOBO for lacking transparency and auditability, leading to distortion and misinformation. It argues that comparisons with U.S. models are inappropriate due to Brazil’s multiparty system and compulsory voting, and emphasizes that election polls measure voter intentions at a given moment, not future behavior, serving as tools to detect trends—not forecasts.
